alternator.description: Converts mechanical energy to electrical energy to charge the battery and power electrical systems
alternator.location: Front of engine, driven by serpentine belt

battery.description: Stores electrical energy to start the engine and power accessories when engine is off
battery.location: Engine bay, usually driver or passenger side
battery.safety: Disconnect negative terminal first. Avoid sparks near battery (hydrogen gas risk)

brake caliper.description: Hydraulic clamp that squeezes brake pads against the rotor to slow the vehicle
brake caliper.location: Behind each wheel, mounted to the steering knuckle
brake caliper.safety: Support vehicle on jack stands. Never work under a vehicle on a jack alone

brake disc.description: Metal disc that rotates with the wheel; brake pads clamp against it to create friction
brake disc.safety: Ensure vehicle is securely supported before removal

coolant reservoir.description: Overflow tank that stores excess coolant as the engine heats and cools
coolant reservoir.location: Engine bay, usually near the radiator
coolant reservoir.safety: NEVER open when engine is hot. Pressurized system can cause burns

engine block.description: Main structure of the engine containing cylinders, coolant passages, and oil galleries
engine block.safety: Major repair - requires engine removal in most cases

oil filter.description: Filters contaminants from engine oil to protect internal engine components
oil filter.location: Side or bottom of engine block
oil filter.safety: Hot oil can burn. Let engine cool before changing

radiator.description: Heat exchanger that cools engine coolant using airflow
radiator.location: Front of engine bay, behind the grille
radiator.safety: Never open radiator cap when hot. Allow full cool-down first

serpentine belt.description: Single belt that drives multiple accessories: alternator, power steering, AC compressor, water pump
serpentine belt.location: Front of engine, wraps around multiple pulleys
serpentine belt.safety: Ensure engine is off. Belt can cause serious injury if caught
tools_needed: Belt tensioner tool or long wrench

spark plug.description: Creates electrical spark to ignite the air-fuel mixture in each cylinder
spark plug.location: Top of engine, one per cylinder (screwed into cylinder head)
spark plug.safety: Work on a cool engine. Remove one at a time to avoid mixing up wire order

air filter.description: Filters dust and debris from air entering the engine for combustion
air filter.location: Air filter box, usually top or side of engine bay
tools_needed: None or screwdriver for airbox clips

exhaust manifold.description: Collects exhaust gases from all cylinders and routes them to the exhaust pipe
exhaust manifold.location: Side of engine block, connects to cylinder head
exhaust manifold.safety: Extremely hot during and after operation. Allow full cool-down

starter motor.description: Electric motor that cranks the engine to start it
starter motor.location: Lower side of engine, connects to flywheel/flexplate
starter motor.safety: Disconnect battery before working on starter to prevent accidental engagement

turbocharger.description: Uses exhaust gas energy to compress intake air for more engine power
turbocharger.safety: Extremely hot. Allow extended cool-down before touching

wrench.description: Hand tool for gripping and turning nuts and bolts
wrench.tip: Use the correct size to avoid rounding off bolt heads. Pull toward you rather than pushing away

socket wrench.description: Ratcheting wrench with interchangeable socket heads for different bolt sizes
socket wrench.tip: Listen for the ratchet click direction. Use extensions for hard-to-reach bolts

screwdriver.description: Tool for driving screws. Common types: Phillips (+) and flathead (-)
screwdriver.tip: Use the correct size and type to avoid stripping the screw head

pliers.description: Gripping tool for holding, bending, or cutting wire and small parts
pliers.tip: Do not use pliers on bolts - use a wrench instead to avoid rounding

torque wrench.description: Precision wrench that measures torque applied to a bolt
torque wrench.tip: Always use when specified in service manual. Over-torquing can strip threads or crack parts

jack.description: Hydraulic device for lifting a vehicle off the ground
jack.tip: Always place on designated jack points. NEVER work under a vehicle supported only by a jack

jack stand.description: Static support stand placed under vehicle after lifting with a jack
jack stand.tip: Mandatory safety equipment. Place on solid frame points after jacking

multimeter.description: Electronic test instrument for measuring voltage, current, and resistance
multimeter.tip: Set to correct measurement mode before testing. Check battery voltage: healthy = 12.6V+
